description: Redraw-proof terminal copy: capture the selection at settle into a cache so Ctrl+C / Ctrl+Insert (and a new mouse-only Copy pill) copy reliably after Claude mouse-tracking redraws clear the live xterm selection; execCommand fallback keeps copy working over http/Tailscale. Copy-on-selection stays opt-in/off.
summary: The terminal Copy command (Ctrl+C after selecting text) works again — it had broken because Claude terminal redraws cleared the highlighted text before you could copy it.
spec_impact_justification: Restores the Ctrl+C/Ctrl+Insert copy-over-http behavior FR-01.28 already specifies (regressed by the mouse-tracking redraw clearing the selection + the #186 copy-on-selection default flip). description: OSC 52 becomes the sole terminal copy path: register term.parser.registerOscHandler(52) (terminal-osc52.ts) to decode Claude Code OSC 52 clipboard writes and relay them to the OS clipboard via copyText (execCommand fallback, http-safe); READ requests denied (no clipboard leak). Removes the redundant WebUI copy machinery: Ctrl+C/Ctrl+Insert interception, copy-on-selection + Settings toggle, the redraw cache + Copy pill (iterate-2026-07-06), and the mouse-mode hint. Ctrl+C now passes through as interrupt/SIGINT; paste unchanged.
summary: Terminal copy works again: Claude copies your selection itself (OSC 52) and the WebUI now writes it to your clipboard; the old, conflicting WebUI copy shortcuts were removed.
spec_impact_justification: Changes how FR-01.28 terminal COPY works: from WebUI Ctrl+C interception to relaying Claude own OSC 52 copy (the WebUI dropped OSC 52 so copy silently failed over http). spec.md FR-01.28 updated with the Iterate-2026-07-07 MODIFY clause.

none_reason: Rendering-correctness bug fix within the embedded terminal (FR-01.28); the spec already requires faithful terminal rendering, so there is no spec.md behavior change.
description: Fix recurring WebGL glyph-atlas corruption (transposed/wrong letters in long sessions): clear the render model via a deferred, coalesced term.clearTextureAtlas() on onChangeTextureAtlas + onRemoveTextureAtlasCanvas (not the too-shallow term.refresh, and not onAdd which would feedback-loop).

Stop forwarding RIGHT-button mouse reports to the pty. Claude Code treats a reported right-click as paste (from its own copy buffer), so a right-click pasted ON TOP OF the browser context-menu Paste that the WebUI relays = double-paste. New terminal-mouse-report.ts isRightButtonMouseReport() filters the EmbeddedTerminal onData sink; right-click is now browser-only (menu -> Paste = one path). Left/middle button + wheel still forwarded (Claude selection/clicks/scroll unaffected).
summary: Right-click in the terminal no longer pastes twice: the right-click is no longer sent to Claude (which pasted on it), so only the browser Paste inserts once.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix restoring single-paste on right-click; FR-01.28 does not specify that a right-click is forwarded to the app. No FR contract change — right-click becomes browser-only (menu Paste), which is what the user expects.

spec_impact_justification: Docs-only condensation of CLAUDE.md; no runtime behavior, API, or UI surface changed; doc-sync meta-test green.
none_reason: Agent-context documentation maintenance; no FR behavior touched.

description: single-session design-gate mockup review hosting (FR-01.45)
summary: When a single-session pipeline pauses at the design step, the Command Center now shows a Review-mockups button that opens the design's own mockup viewer full-screen inside the app and saves your per-round feedback straight into the project, then Resume applies it.

description: DELETE/cascade tears down the live pty before wiping scrollback/snapshot; SnapshotStore.clear() fences the per-task write queue before unlink (privacy hardening, MEDIUM-B1 / CLAUDE.md rule 20)
summary: Deleting a task now shuts down its live terminal before erasing that terminal's saved history, and the erase waits for any in-flight save to finish, so a secret shown in the terminal can no longer linger on disk after a delete.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix that restores the privacy behavior the FRs already intend (FR-01.09 task DELETE, FR-01.25 project-delete cascade best-effort scrollback+snapshot clear, FR-01.28 pty kill + scrollback/snapshot privacy). No FR's observable HTTP/UI contract changes; the fix corrects a disk/process-table side-effect only, so no spec.md edit is required.

description: WS re-attach with no live pty now resolves the persisted disk snapshot BEFORE spawn() (preSpawnReplay in ws-upgrade-handler.ts), so the fresh empty HeadlessMirror can no longer shadow tryReadSnapshot; terminal history is replayed after any pty death. Live-pty resolves in the onOpen IIFE (ADR-092 live-first). pty-manager untouched; net-zero LOC (F02 / D02).
summary: Re-opening a task's embedded terminal after its shell had died (server restart, Stop-session, idle timeout, or shell exit) now restores the saved terminal history instead of showing a blank shell; a live terminal still takes precedence over the saved copy.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix that restores the replay-on-attach behavior FR-01.28 already intends (WS upgrade onOpen replays the persisted terminal snapshot). No FR's observable HTTP/UI contract changes — the WS envelope shape and every rendered surface are unchanged; the fix corrects the internal spawn->resolve ordering so the disk-snapshot fallback is reachable again. No spec.md edit required.

description: win32 Preview dev-server spawn: PATHEXT + cmd.exe /d /s /c wrapper, backslash-safe, shell:false preserved
summary: The Preview button now actually starts a project dev server on Windows instead of silently failing.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix that makes FR-01.17 shell:false Preview spawn work on Windows; no observable FR behavior change and no spec text edit — the button already rendered, the spawn now works.

description: sdk-sessions persist merges under lock (3-way) + atomic tmp+rename - no cross-instance data loss (F08)
summary: Two Command Center windows open at the same time no longer wipe out each other's saved tasks - the app now safely merges its task file instead of overwriting the whole thing.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix restoring the intended no-lost-update and daemon-claim-field-preservation behavior of the sdk-sessions task store; the FR-01.08/FR-01.09 create/patch/delete contract is byte-identical (behavior-preserving from the spec's standpoint, so no spec edit).

description: Repair the two ADR-038 schema E2E guards (62 + 70-g) on an isolated store with an isolation self-lock
summary: Fixed two automated schema-migration tests so they run safely against a throwaway copy of the session store instead of sitting broken or risking the user's real one.
spec_impact_justification: Test-only repair: Playwright e2e specs + isolation helper + playwright config changed; no server/client production code and no observable change to FR-01.08's task-store persistence contract.

description: Transcript poll never resurrects terminal-state tasks; dirty-checked store persist; findByUuid discovery retry
summary: A finished or closed task no longer flips back to an active/other state (and no longer keeps needlessly re-saving the task list every second) when its background transcript check runs, and a momentary file-system hiccup while looking up the log file no longer makes a live task look gone.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving hardening of the transcript-poll state machine + JSONL discovery. Terminal-state immutability + the dirty-checked persist keep FR-01.08 derived-state persistence and FR-01.32 transcript-poll stickiness within their documented contracts (draft-sticky simply extended to done/launch_failed), and the findByUuid retry envelope reinforces FR-01.12 stated EBUSY/EPERM/EACCES/ENOENT retry guarantee. No FR observable contract changed; a bug that silently resurrected terminal tasks is removed.

description: Master-shadow reuse is project-scoped (F06) + a direct-API masterRun launch stamps parentRunMaster+runId so the double-master guard sees it (F34)
summary: When two projects came from a copied folder, launching the pipeline for the second one could reopen the first one's session; now each project launches its own and a duplicate master launch is properly blocked.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix within FR-01.01 single-session master-run behavior (project-scoped shadow reuse + the 409 master_run_already_attached guard). No FR text change; corrects an under-keyed identity match on the client hook and the server launch branch.

description: ProjectManager.load() now tolerates a corrupt / truncated / empty projects.json: parse errors quarantine the file aside (.corrupt-<ts>-<uuid>) and boot continues with an empty registry instead of exiting FATAL on every restart; persist() writes atomically via tmp+rename to shrink the corruption window at the source. Logic factored into core/project-registry-io.ts (F07).
summary: If the projects-list file got cut off mid-write (for example when the dev server is force-killed with taskkill), the Command Center used to crash on every startup. It now recovers on its own: the damaged file is moved aside for safekeeping and the app starts with an empty project list, and future saves are written safely so the file can't be left half-written.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix within FR-01.24 (project registry GET/POST: the projects.json load + persist path) and FR-01.25 (project detail/edit/delete, which shares the same persist). No FR text change: adds corrupt-file tolerance to ProjectManager.load() plus atomic tmp+rename to its persist, mirroring the sdk-sessions-store recovery pattern already in the codebase.

description: PUT /api/external/projects/:id/file validated the .md/.markdown allowlist on the pathGuard string-resolved path (the symlink's NAME) while the write followed the link to its realpath; realPathGuard only re-checked CONTAINMENT, so a within-root .md-named symlink (notes.md -> shipwright_run_config.json / package.json / a build script) bypassed the markdown-only write boundary and the read-only run-config invariant, up to code-execution via a build-script/git-hook target. Fix: lstat the target (does not follow the final component) and reject any final-component symlink with a distinct 403 symlink_forbidden; the read+rename write target is now the requested path (not the realpath) so renameSync replaces the final directory entry and can never dereference a TOCTOU-swapped symlink. realPathGuard is retained as defense-in-depth for a parent-directory symlink escape (F09).
summary: A project file that is secretly a shortcut (symlink) with a .md name can no longer be used to overwrite a non-markdown file (like the run config) through the in-app markdown editor - the save is now refused outright.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix within FR-01.34 (SmartViewer markdown editor PUT /file write surface). No FR text change: the FR's intended behavior - writes are restricted to markdown files inside the project - is preserved and now correctly enforced against a .md-named symlink that pointed at a non-markdown target. Adds an lstat symlink rejection + a TOCTOU-safe rename target; the markdown allowlist, ETag/If-Match concurrency, and atomic write are unchanged.

description: readRunConfig's existence stat probe had no retry and no last-good-cache fallback for a non-ENOENT fault, so a Windows rename-window EPERM/EBUSY/EACCES (the orchestrator rewrites shipwright_run_config.json atomically on every phase_task transition) returned {status:"invalid"} immediately, escaping the torn-read mitigations the read/parse path already had; compounding it, LAST_GOOD_TTL_MS (5000) equalled the client poll cadence (5000) so the read/parse cache fallback was always expired at fallback time. Client-side runConfigPollIntervalMs returned false for invalid, latching TanStack Query polling OFF, so one torn poll made the Pipelines lane silently vanish mid-run and never recover without a manual refocus. Fix: the stat probe now runs through the same retryTornRead envelope + last-good cache as the read path (helper extracted to the run-config-v2 types module because the reader is at its 439-LOC bloat ceiling, parseRunMode precedent; SERVER mirror only, no client-bundle pollution); LAST_GOOD_TTL_MS raised to 30000 (comfortably above the 5s cadence); and the client keeps polling on invalid at a 10s backoff so a transient flap self-heals. Stale-serve stays bounded to physical faults only (a malformed-but-readable file returns invalid immediately) (F15).
summary: The pipeline progress card no longer disappears from the board when a project's run-config file is briefly locked mid-run (a normal Windows hiccup while the run rewrites it). The Command Center now retries the brief interruption, keeps the last good copy for 30 seconds, and keeps checking on a light schedule so the pipeline lane heals on its own instead of vanishing until you switch windows.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix within FR-01.18 (Read shipwright_run_config.json GET: read-only forwarder with per-row fault isolation, torn-read retry, last-good cache) and FR-01.01 (Task board Pipelines lane, whose SingleSessionRunCard/MasterTaskCard rendering is driven by the run-config poll). No FR text change: the fix restores FR-01.18's documented intent (a torn read never produces an invalid flap visible to the UI) that the 5s-TTL == 5s-poll-cadence bug had defeated - the stat probe is brought under the same retry+cache guarantee as the read path and the TTL is raised above the poll cadence so the cache actually functions; the client stops latching so FR-01.01's lane no longer vanishes on a one-poll transient. Read-only-observer invariants (CLAUDE.md rules 1/12, DO-NOT #12) preserved - no writes to shipwright_run_config.json or ~/.claude/projects/**.

description: scripts/dev-restart.js findPidsOnPorts discovered kill-target PIDs with unanchored substring matching: Windows ran `netstat -ano -p TCP | findstr :<port>` and grabbed the trailing PID off every matching line - findstr is a plain whole-line substring match with no state filter, so it caught (1) ESTABLISHED browser sockets whose FOREIGN address ended :<port> (the user's open Command Center tab connected to Vite) and (2) port-prefix collisions (:5173 is a substring of :51730), then taskkill /F /T killed those unrelated PIDs including the browser tree; POSIX ran `lsof -ti tcp:<ports>` with no state filter so every socket incl. ESTABLISHED browser connections on those ports was killed. Fix: a new exported parseWindowsListenerPids(netstatOutput, ports) in kill-targets.js splits each row into columns and requires proto startsWith('TCP') + state LISTENING + an EXACT local-address port match (port = digits after the last colon, so :51730 != :5173 and [::]:5173 parses), then takes the PID column, dedups, tolerates empty/malformed input; a new buildLsofCommand(ports) emits `lsof -ti -sTCP:LISTEN tcp:<ports>` (POSIX state filter) and coerces ports to positive integers before interpolation; dev-restart.js findPidsOnPorts now runs one plain `netstat -ano` read parsed structurally on Windows (dropping -p TCP, which EXCLUDES IPv6 [::] listeners that Vite/Node bind by default - empirically verified) and buildLsofCommand on POSIX (F16).
summary: Running the dev-server restart command (npm run dev:fresh) no longer risks killing your open browser tab or an unrelated program that happens to use a similar port number - it now targets only the actual dev-server processes that are truly listening on the two configured ports, including IPv6 ones.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix in the developer-tooling dev-restart helper (npm run dev:fresh) within the FR-01.31 dev-server-config domain (PORT/VITE_PORT + worktree-parallel port scope). No FR text change and no Command Center product behavior change: the fix tightens WHICH processes the local restart script kills (LISTENING + exact-port only, IPv6-inclusive) so it stops over-matching the user's browser and port-prefix collisions - it does not touch any server route, client component, or user-facing surface. Read-only-observer invariants (CLAUDE.md rules 1/12) unaffected; the script writes into no ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json.

description: Strip the webui's own PORT/VITE_PORT/HONO_HOST from the embedded-terminal pty env (buildSpawnEnv) so a PORT-honouring dev server started in the terminal cannot collide with the webui
summary: A dev server you start inside the embedded terminal no longer accidentally inherits the Command Center's own port (3847) and clashes with it.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving hardening: the pty still spawns the same whitelisted shell (ADR-067) with the same markers; only the webui's leaked operational network vars are removed from the child env. FR-01.28's observable terminal contract is unchanged - this is an env-leak fix, not a spec change.

description: install-windows.ps1 wrote the login-autostart VBS launcher (start-server.vbs) with Set-Content -Encoding ASCII, which maps every char >127 to '?' - so a repo cloned under a non-ASCII path (umlauts / accents / CJK, e.g. under C:\Users\<accented-name>\Documents\shipwright-webui) got a corrupted $ServerDir / $serverEntryPoint baked into the launcher, silently breaking the login autostart while the installer reported 'Installation complete!'. Fix: write the VBS with -Encoding Unicode (UTF-16LE + BOM, read natively by wscript.exe); compute the backslash-escaped path ONCE and reuse it in both the VBS generator and a new post-write round-trip guard that re-reads the file (Get-Content -Encoding Unicode) and aborts loudly (removes the bad launcher, exit 1, no Startup shortcut) if the embedded $ServerDir did not survive the encoding.
summary: The Windows 'start on login' installer no longer breaks when your Command Center folder path contains accented or non-English characters; the launcher is now saved in a Unicode format Windows reads natively, and the installer stops with a clear error instead of a false success if the folder path could not be preserved.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix in the install-windows login-autostart launcher, which FR-01.07 explicitly names as a consumer ('Liveness probe used by dev_server.py, smoke tests, and the install-windows autostart'). The encoding fix ensures the autostarted server - whose liveness FR-01.07's health check probes - actually starts on a non-ASCII repo path instead of silently failing. No FR text change and no Command Center product behavior change: no server route, client component, or user-facing runtime surface is touched; the health-check GET contract and response shape are unchanged. Read-only-observer invariants (CLAUDE.md rules 1/12) unaffected - the installer writes only into %USERPROFILE%\.shipwright-webui\ and the Startup folder.

description: start-server-production.ps1 and stop-server.ps1 hardcoded port 3847 for the kill sweep and the readiness poll while their .sh twins honor $PORT via PORT="${PORT:-3847}". A Windows operator on a custom PORT (a documented env override in CLAUDE.md; repo-root .env.local, loaded by the launched node via --env-file-if-exists, can carry PORT) got the OLD server on the custom port surviving the stop/restart, EADDRINUSE on the new one, a wrong 'did NOT come up on port 3847' diagnosis, and a skipped claude.json re-heal. Fix: both scripts derive $Port once from $env:PORT with a 3847 default, guarded by a 5-digit numeric match so an unset/blank/non-numeric/overflowing PORT degrades to 3847 instead of throwing on the [int] cast, and thread $Port through the kill sweep (-LocalPort $Port), the readiness poll, the launched node child env (start script: a child-scoped set PORT prefix on the cmd launch, mirroring the .sh PORT="$PORT" node prefix without mutating the parent shell), and every operator message.
summary: The Windows production start/stop scripts now honor a custom server port (the PORT setting) instead of always assuming 3847, so restarting or stopping the Command Center on a non-default port works and reports the right port - matching the Mac/Linux scripts.
spec_impact_justification: Bug fix in the Windows production operator scripts (start-server-production.ps1 / stop-server.ps1) that start, stop, and readiness-poll the Hono server. FR-01.07 (health check / liveness probe) names the install-windows autostart + smoke tests as consumers of the server's liveness; these scripts perform the equivalent port-bound readiness check on launch. The fix makes that check target the operator's actual PORT instead of a hardcoded 3847 - no FR text change and no Command Center product behavior change: no server route, client component, or user-facing runtime surface is touched. Read-only-observer invariants (CLAUDE.md rules 1/12) are unaffected - the scripts write only into the user's .shipwright-webui folder and never into Claude's projects JSONL or shipwright_run_config.json.

description: useTaskTranscript's terminal-state stop condition read result.task from the effect's mount-time closure (always the initial null), so a done/launch_failed task polled the stateless transcript endpoint (FR-01.12) forever (F21); and TaskDetailHeader mounted a SECOND independent 1 Hz useTaskTranscript solely to regex the latest model name, doubling full-JSONL disk reads + payloads + locked sdk-sessions.json persists per open tab (F22). Fix: track the freshest server-reported task state in a ref the tick reads (so the terminal-state stop actually fires) and reset that ref per effect run so a prior done task's state never leaks into a newly-selected running task (external-review HIGH); and remove the header's own poller - TaskDetailPage's single poller now derives modelName via the pure extractModelName helper and passes it to the header as a prop.
summary: A finished task's Transcript view now stops re-reading the session file once the task is done instead of polling forever, and each open task page makes one transcript request per second instead of two - the model label in the header still shows, it just comes from the single shared poller now.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving fix to the client transcript poller (useTaskTranscript) that consumes FR-01.12 (stateless byte-range transcript read GET). The transcript still displays, the header still shows the model label, and the byte-range poll contract/cadence is unchanged - the poller merely (1) stops when the task reaches a terminal state exactly as its own docblock already promised, and (2) no longer runs a duplicate second instance per page. No FR text change, no server route or user-facing component behavior change, no new write surface. Read-only-observer invariants (CLAUDE.md rules 1/12) intact - useTaskTranscript still only issues GET /transcript and writes nothing into ~/.claude/projects/** or shipwright_run_config.json.

description: ScrollbackStore.sweepExpired evaluated file freshness per-file in readdir order while mutating the group map, so a fresh <id>.log visited before an expired <id>.log.1 (common alphabetical order) ran a no-op delete then let the expired sibling re-add the group and get unlinked - the documented 'skip the whole task if any file is fresh' rule failed. Fix: two-pass - bucket every file by taskId (OR-accumulate hasFresh), then unlink only all-expired groups; order-independent. Removed the dead oldestMtime field; added an optional readdirFn test hook (mirrors renameFn/now).
summary: The daily cleanup of old terminal scrollback no longer deletes a still-active task's archived history just because the files were listed in a certain order.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving bug fix: restores FR-01.28 AC(e)'s documented 'active-task-aware skip / skip the whole task if any file is fresh' guarantee for the TTL sweep. No AC, endpoint, or contract changed - the sweep's intended semantics are unchanged; only the order-dependent implementation defect is removed.

description: The shared Hono errorHandler (global app.onError) only branched on AppError, so proper-lockfile ELOCKED contention on ~8 store-mutating endpoints that call store.persist() without an inline try/catch (fork, close, create, launch, DELETE task, inbox dismiss, settings PUT, project-delete cascade) and a SyntaxError from an unguarded c.req.json() (projects POST/PATCH, settings PUT) both surfaced as opaque 500s. Fix: two central branches, after AppError and before the 500 fallback - (err as NodeJS.ErrnoException).code === 'ELOCKED' -> the same retryable 409 body the per-route handlers emit ({error:'sdk-sessions.json is locked, retry'}); err instanceof SyntaxError && /json/i.test(err.message) -> 400 invalid_json. Per-route handlers still short-circuit first so pinned contracts are untouched; the /json/i guard keeps a server-side non-body SyntaxError (regex/Function) as a truthful 500. F29 + F33.
summary: Lock-contention failures on task/project/settings actions now return a retryable 'locked, retry' (409) and malformed request bodies return 'invalid request body' (400), instead of a misleading generic 500.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving error-contract hardening: the central errorHandler now surfaces proper-lockfile ELOCKED as the retryable 409 that FR-01.09 already documents (extending it to the sibling store-mutating endpoints that previously leaked 500), and unguarded request-body parse failures as the established 400 invalid_json contract, instead of opaque 500s. No AC, endpoint set, or documented status is changed - the per-route handlers still short-circuit first; only the previously-uncovered 500 leaks are corrected. DO-NOT rule 6 (multi-writer state files use proper-lockfile; PATCH surfaces ELOCKED as 409) preserved and extended.

description: Two launch branches decided fresh-launch vs resume from the STALE persisted firstJsonlObservedAt, so a launch issued while the pre-bound <uuid>.jsonl already existed re-emitted --session-id and Claude rejected it ('Session ID already in use'). Fix: before building the command, both branches probe on-disk JSONL existence for the pre-bound uuid via SessionWatcher.findByUuid (filename-first, rule 3; short-circuited when the stamp is already present) and emit --resume regardless of the client flag, stamping firstJsonlObservedAt on discovery. master-run-branch.ts (F14): the ~5-15s first-write window where the board CTA sends resume:false -> --resume <masterUuid> (no slash command). phase-task-branch.ts (F28): re-Continue after a pre-claim crash (phase_task still awaiting_launch) -> --resume, dropping the slash command (Claude already holds the phase conversation). Wired via a new optional jsonlExistsOnDisk dep on LaunchRouterDeps, sourced from the watcher in external/routes.ts. Client (useLaunchMasterRun.ts + MasterRunLaunchButton.tsx): new shared masterShadowIsEstablished() derives resume + CTA label from firstJsonlObservedAt OR the live lastJsonlSeenMtimeMs overlay (GET /tasks, ADR-102) so the label flips to Resume the instant the transcript hits disk. Legacy new-plain resume semantics untouched (fix localized to the two branches). Rules 1 (launcher only builds strings) + 9 (--plugin-dir re-passed on the resume shape) + read-only-observer 1/12 preserved. F14 + F28.
summary: Re-opening a running pipeline from the board no longer fails with Claude's 'Session ID already in use' - the app now checks the conversation file on disk and correctly builds a Resume command (instead of a rejected fresh-start) during the brief window after a launch, for both single-session master runs and multi-session phase re-continues.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving launch-command hardening: the launch route already had the resume-vs-fresh concept (FR-01.11 resume gate, FR-01.10 launch-command shape); D18 makes that decision disk-truthful (probe <uuid>.jsonl) instead of trusting the stale persisted firstJsonlObservedAt, and aligns the FR-01.01 single-session board CTA label with it. No AC, endpoint, or documented behavior changes - only the previously-broken duplicate --session-id launch (which Claude rejected) is corrected to the --resume it should always have been. Read-only-observer rules 1/12 intact (no run_config/JSONL writes); rules 1 + 9 preserved.

description: sweep orphaned <taskId>.snapshot.tmp-* staging files (boot + 24h periodic + delete-cascade)
summary: Interrupted terminal-snapshot writes no longer leak secret-bearing staging files to disk forever; a boot and daily sweep plus task-delete now reclaim any orphaned staging file older than an hour.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving disk hygiene for FR-01.28: adds reclamation of orphaned .snapshot.tmp-* strays via a boot + 24h sweep and the delete cascade. No observable terminal/replay/spawn behavior changes; live .snapshot and .log files are preserved.

description: preview child lifecycle: drain stdio, dedup spawn, tree-kill + await-exit (F11/F12/F13)
summary: The Preview dev server no longer freezes, double-launches, or leaves stray background processes behind when you open or switch previews.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving robustness of the existing Preview dev-server feature (FR-01.17): no observable contract change. The child is now drained, deduplicated, and tree-killed instead of piped-but-unread, double-spawned, and leaf-killed.

description: preview config validation: host-pin the returned URL + reject an invalid dev_server.port before spawn (F10/F30)
summary: Opening a Preview can no longer be redirected off your machine by a hand-authored project profile, and a profile whose dev-server port is missing now fails fast with a clear message instead of hanging for a minute and then killing the running dev server.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving hardening of the existing Preview dev-server feature (FR-01.17): a valid profile's observable contract is unchanged (same button, same /preview route, same returned-URL shape for a normal ready_path). It corrects two defects reachable only via a malicious/incomplete profile - an off-host window.open redirect (F10) and a portless-profile 60s-probe-then-kill-healthy-server (F30).

description: validate task titles at create + fork like PATCH does — reject embedded newlines (CR/LF/CRLF) + over-length via a shared normalizeTitle helper, and validate fork before child creation so no orphan row is left (F27)
summary: Creating or forking a task with a broken title (embedded line breaks or an absurdly long name) is now rejected with a clear error instead of silently saving a task that then crashes every time you try to launch or resume it.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving hardening of the existing task create/fork/edit endpoints (FR-01.08 / FR-01.09): a valid title's observable contract is unchanged (same 200, same trimmed title, same 'Untitled task' / '<parent> - fork' default for a blank title). It only brings POST /tasks and POST /tasks/:id/fork into line with the title validation PATCH already enforces, closing an inconsistency where an automated writer (run-config phase title, triage fix-now seed, leadwright daemon) could persist a newline/over-length title that later 500s every Launch/Resume and left an orphan fork child.

description: boot coherence check treats empty stack.frontend {} as backend-only (F32)
summary: The Command Center no longer prints a false preview-misconfigured warning at startup for backend-only projects that intentionally have no frontend.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving diagnostic fix for FR-01.17 preview-capability gate: an intentionally-empty stack.frontend {} paired with dev_server:null is now read as backend-only, so the boot-time coherence check stops emitting a spurious warning. The PreviewButton visibility gate (stack.frontend AND dev_server.command AND actions.preview.enabled) is unchanged — the button was already hidden for this profile; only the noisy startup log is removed.

description: Share the ADR-096 preserve-gate across the finalize + flush snapshot write paths so the last-detach flush no longer clobbers a richer disk snapshot
summary: The embedded terminal now keeps its scrollback history when you switch away and back to a task more than once - the automatic save on leaving no longer overwrites a fuller saved copy with an emptier one.
spec_impact_justification: Behavior-preserving bug fix that restores the intended FR-01.28 replay contract (terminal scrollback survives repeated detach/reopen). No FR wording changes: the on-disk snapshot format is byte-identical; only which payload is persisted (the preserve-larger decision, already present in finalize) is now also applied on the last-detach flush.

none_reason: The fix restores behavior FR-01.28 already describes (the embedded terminal renders the buffer it holds). No FR row, AC or wording changes.
description: Heal the WebGL glyph atlas on a re-show (window focus/visibility/bfcache restore + Transcript-Terminal tab activation), not only on xterm's own atlas-mutation events. term.refresh routes to WebglRenderer._updateModel, which skips cells that look unchanged and clears neither the render model nor the glyph texture; a browser that evicts or repacks the GPU texture of a backgrounded window emits no atlas event and does not lose the context, so nothing healed it. The existing #206 fence is now exposed as handle.healAtlas and fired from every trailing activation pass, gated on the terminal being visible, retracted on GPU context loss.

none_reason: Ops tooling only (deploy shell scripts + two Node helpers + two CI guard tests). No FR behavior changes: no route, store, WS handler, UI or message contract is touched.
description: BUG fix: the production deploy killed the server from inside the process it was about to kill (embedded-terminal descendant), so the new server never started. Kill+start+readiness+heal moved into a detached swapper.

none_reason: Behavior restoration, not a behavior change. The More-options panel was already specified to render its fields when expanded; a flexbox rule was silently clipping them. No FR text changes: the intended behavior is unchanged, only now actually delivered. The triage auto-expand is explicitly PRESERVED.
description: Create dialog: More-options panel was rendered clipped (fields sliced, no scrollbar) when the dialog opened from a triage item. Root cause: ModalShell's bounded column-flex body let its overflow-hidden child (MoreOptionsDisclosure) lose its flex automatic minimum size, so flexbox squeezed the panel below its content and it clipped itself - and, having absorbed the negative free space, the body never became scrollable, leaving the fields unreachable. Fix: pin [&>*]:shrink-0 on the scroll body so children keep their natural height and the container scrolls.

none_reason: Behavior-preserving refactor. No user-visible behavior changes: ModalShell renders an identical class set, and the four other containers gain only [&>*]:shrink-0, verified a no-op (no direct child in any of them declares flex-1/shrink/basis). It converges an existing invariant and adds a ratchet; no FR text changes.
description: Converge the bounded-scroll-container invariant.
